cases.title: TS-20: Successful POST /check-in response latency meets p95 ≤ 2000 ms under normal network
preconditions: API and Compose PostgreSQL running per 13-docker-compose-local-runtime.md
Active session with room GPS (10.762622, 106.660172) and enrolled Student with Pending attendance
Valid unexpired QrToken from GET /api/v1/sessions/:sessionId/qr/current
Student authenticated with in-radius coordinates (10.762700, 106.660200)
Normal network profile (no artificial throttling)
steps: Execute at least 20 sequential POST /api/v1/check-in requests with unique enrolled students and valid tokens each
Record wall-clock latency from request start to full response body received for each HTTP 200 Success
Compute p95 latency across successful responses
Assert p95 ≤ 2000 ms
Assert each Success response contains outcome Success and attendance.status Present
cases.expected: Testing-plan §3 TS-20 satisfied: check-in API p95 ≤ 2 s under normal pilot network per NFR-04 and AC-07a confirmation SLA

cases.title: 150 parallel unique-student check-ins achieve p95 ≤ 2000 ms with error rate < 0.1%
preconditions: ConcurrentCheckIn load fixture per testing-plan §7 Peak concurrent check-in
Active session with 150 enrolled students each with Pending attendance and unique Valid QrToken
Real PostgreSQL via Compose; API or CheckInService invocable in parallel
Staggered submission window 0–5 minutes; normal network profile
steps: Launch 150 parallel POST /api/v1/check-in requests (one per unique studentId) with in-radius coordinates
Record response latency and HTTP status for each request
Compute p95 latency across HTTP 200 Success responses
Compute error rate as (non-200 responses + business failures) / 150
Assert p95 ≤ 2000 ms and error rate < 0.1%
cases.expected: NFR-04 verification method satisfied: load test with 150 concurrent users meets p95 ≤ 2 s target per 07-non-functional-risk §2.2 and testing-plan §7

cases.title: CheckInService.submit completes PostgreSQL transaction within 2000 ms at module boundary
preconditions: Real PostgreSQL test database running via Compose
Active session with enrolled student Pending attendance and Valid qr_tokens row
CheckInService wired to AttendanceRecordRepository and QrTokenRepository
High-resolution timer available around CheckInService.submit invocation
steps: Record timestamp T0 immediately before CheckInService.submit(tokenId, locationPayload, studentId)
Invoke submit with valid in-radius coordinates
Record timestamp T1 when submit promise resolves with outcome Success
Assert T1 minus T0 ≤ 2000 ms
Query attendance_records and qr_tokens and assert Present + Consumed persisted
cases.expected: Module-level check-in submission including DB commit completes within NFR-04 latency budget excluding client network overhead

cases.title: POST /api/v1/check-in returns HTTP 200 Success within 2000 ms for valid submission
preconditions: API running on port 3001 against healthy Compose PostgreSQL
Authenticated enrolled Student session cookie set
Active sessionId with Valid QrToken and student Pending attendance
In-radius coordinates supplied in request body
steps: Record T0 before sending POST /api/v1/check-in with body { tokenId, latitude: 10.762700, longitude: 106.660200, spoofMetadata: { mockLocationDetected: false, platform: "android" } }
Await HTTP response and record T1 when response body fully received
Assert HTTP status 200 OK
Assert response body outcome Success with attendance.status Present
Assert T1 minus T0 ≤ 2000 ms
cases.expected: HTTP 200 Success contract delivered within NFR-04 p95 target for single check-in submission under normal conditions

cases.title: Flow A step 4: enrolled student check-in completes within 2000 ms in workshop lifecycle
preconditions: Flow A steps 1–3 complete: roster seeded, session opened Active, QR token polled via GET /sessions/:id/qr/current
GET /api/v1/check-in/tokens/:tokenId/preflight returns 200 outcome Valid (preflight passes per AC-07a)
Authenticated enrolled Student session with in-radius GPS fixture (10.762700, 106.660200) for room (10.762622, 106.660172)
Normal network; full E2E stack on ports 3000/3001 with Compose db healthy
steps: Record T0 before POST /api/v1/check-in with tokenId from Flow A step 3 and in-radius coordinates
Assert HTTP 200 outcome Success with attendance Present
Record T1 when response received
Assert T1 minus T0 ≤ 2000 ms
Instructor GET /api/v1/sessions/:sessionId/attendance shows present count incremented within same request window
cases.expected: Flow A happy-path check-in step meets NFR-04 latency SLA tied to AC-07a confirmation within 2 s under normal network

cases.title: CheckInTransaction integration suite measures submit duration against 2000 ms budget on real Postgres
preconditions: CheckInTransaction integration suite per testing-plan §5 against Compose PostgreSQL
Active session fixture with Pending attendance and Valid QrToken
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://wecheck:wecheck@localhost:5432/wecheck
Harness docker-compose-db slice acceptance tag NFR-04
steps: Run integration test harness wrapping CheckInService.submit with performance assertion hook
Execute submit for valid enrolled student with in-radius payload
Assert transaction commit completes within 2000 ms measured at service boundary
Assert attendance Present and qr_token Consumed after timing assertion passes
Store latency sample in test report artifact for pilot APM baseline comparison
cases.expected: Integration layer validates NFR-04 at DB/module boundary using real Postgres per 11-testing-plan §5 and 13-docker-compose §5.2 persistence policy

cases.title: Mobile check-in success confirmation UI visible within 2000 ms of submit tap
preconditions: Authenticated Student on mobile viewport 375×812 with GPS permission granted
Active session with valid check-in deep link /check-in?token=:tokenId&session=:sessionId
Preflight already passed and GpsCaptureStep in ready state (Vị trí đã sẵn sàng per AC-08f)
Auth user prefetched on mount so submit timing excludes duplicate auth round-trip
Normal network profile (no throttling); web on port 3000, API healthy
In-radius geolocation mocked or granted (10.762700, 106.660200) for room (10.762622, 106.660172)
steps: Navigate to /check-in deep link on mobile viewport
Wait for preflight pass and GPS ready state with no Spinner and submit enabled (AC-08f)
Record timestamp T0 immediately before clicking Xác nhận điểm danh submit control
Click submit and wait for success confirmation element (Điểm danh thành công or Present badge)
Record timestamp T1 when success UI is visible
Assert T1 minus T0 ≤ 2000 ms
cases.expected: Student confirmation UI within 2 seconds of submit tap under normal conditions per NFR-04 target and AC-07a; server-authoritative outcome reflected in UI

cases.title: Mobile check-in page shows loading state during submit without premature success before API completes
preconditions: Authenticated Student on mobile viewport with valid check-in deep link
Preflight passed; GPS step in ready state (Vị trí đã sẵn sàng) per AC-08f
GPS permission granted with in-radius coordinates (10.762700, 106.660200)
Network request to POST /api/v1/check-in observable in browser devtools or Playwright network log
steps: Navigate to /check-in page and wait for preflight pass and GPS ready state
Click Xác nhận điểm danh submit control
Assert Spinner visible and submit disabled while POST /api/v1/check-in is in-flight (AC-08g submitting state)
Assert success confirmation (Điểm danh thành công) appears only after network response completes
Measure elapsed time from submit click to success UI and assert ≤ 2000 ms under normal network
cases.expected: UI respects server authority and NFR-04 timing: AC-08g submitting spinner during API call; confirmation appears after successful response within 2 s budget per ui-states §4 slow-network guidance

cases.title: Load test gate fails when measured p95 exceeds 2000 ms (NFR-04 boundary)
preconditions: Load test harness (k6, Artillery, or equivalent) configured per testing-plan §7
150 virtual users with valid check-in payloads against Active session
Baseline environment documented in docs/test-reports/load-test-YYYY-MM-DD.md template
steps: Run peak concurrent check-in scenario with 150 VUs staggered 0–5 min
Collect latency histogram for HTTP 200 Success responses
Compute p95 latency and error rate
If p95 > 2000 ms or error rate ≥ 0.1%, mark test FAIL and record remediation plan
If p95 ≤ 2000 ms and error rate < 0.1%, mark test PASS for NFR-04 pilot gate
cases.expected: NFR-04 pass/fail boundary enforced at p95 = 2000 ms and error rate < 0.1% per 07-non-functional-risk §2.2 verification and testing-plan §7 pass criteria

cases.title: Under 500 concurrent users multi-session stress, check-in p95 remains ≤ 3000 ms (NFR-23 ceiling) without breaching NFR-04 pilot target on 150 VU slice
preconditions: Multi-session stress configuration per testing-plan §7: 500 concurrent users across 4 Active sessions
Subset of 150 VUs dedicated to single-session NFR-04 measurement
Full-preview stack with PostgreSQL, API, and web containers healthy
steps: Launch 500 concurrent check-in requests across 4 sessions
Isolate latency samples from the 150-user single-session cohort
Assert 150-user cohort p95 ≤ 2000 ms (NFR-04 pilot target)
Assert overall 500-user p95 ≤ 3000 ms per NFR-23
Assert combined error rate < 0.1%
cases.expected: NFR-04 pilot latency target holds for 150-user peak while broader stress test validates scalability headroom per testing-plan §7 multi-session row

cases.title: POST /api/v1/check-in business rejection (409 DuplicateCheckIn) still responds within 2000 ms
preconditions: Student already Present for Active session
Fresh Valid QrToken available
Authenticated Student session with in-radius coordinates
steps: Record T0 before POST /api/v1/check-in with fresh tokenId for already-Present student
Assert HTTP status 409 with outcome DuplicateCheckIn
Record T1 when response body received
Assert T1 minus T0 ≤ 2000 ms
Verify fast rejection does not block subsequent valid check-ins for other students
cases.expected: Error-path check-in responses remain within NFR-04 latency budget; slow duplicate rejection would degrade cohort completion per NFR-05 risk

cases.title: Integration load sample: 20 CheckInService.submit calls report median ≤ 1000 ms and p95 ≤ 2000 ms
preconditions: Real PostgreSQL with 20 enrolled students Pending on Active session
20 unique Valid QrTokens
CheckInService directly invocable without HTTP overhead
steps: Execute 20 sequential CheckInService.submit calls with unique studentId each
Record service-boundary duration for each Success outcome
Compute median and p95 across samples
Assert median ≤ 1000 ms (operational headroom below NFR-04 ceiling)
Assert p95 ≤ 2000 ms
cases.expected: Module integration layer provides latency headroom below NFR-04 p95 ceiling before HTTP and mobile client overhead applied
